name: Cheese Fondue-rec.foods
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 3 cups of mild cheddar cheese (shredded) with 1 cup of shredded Swiss cheese.
2. Mix in 1/2 cup of beer, 1 teaspoon of minced garlic, 1 teaspoon of dried mustard, and 1 teaspoon of Worcestershire sauce. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
3. In a covered plastic bowl, mix the cheeses and toss with a small handful of flour to coat (this helps the cheese melt nicely without clumps).
4. Heat the beer in a fondue pot or saucepan until it is steaming but not boiling.
5. Slowly stir in the cheese mixture into the hot beer, constantly stirring to prevent lumps. If the sauce becomes too thick, add more beer to thin it to your desired consistency.
6. Add garlic, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, and salt and pepper, then mix well.
7. Lower the heat to maintain a gentle simmer.
8. Dip bread chunks (rye, French, Italian, or pumpernickel), apple pieces (MacIntosh and Granny Smith are good choices), carrot slices, cauliflower florets, and other raw vegetables into the cheese sauce.
